practice.name: Problem 25 (2019 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Alice has 12 apples. In how many ways can she share them with Becky and Chris so that each of the three people has at least two apples?

practice.name: Problem 20 (2026 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The land of Catania uses gold coins and silver coins. Gold coins are $1$ mm thick and silver coins are $3$ mm thick. In how many ways can Taylor make a stack of coins that is $8$ mm tall using any arrangement of gold and silver coins, assuming order matters?

practice.name: Problem 11 (2016 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Determine how many two-digit numbers satisfy the following property: When the number is added to the number obtained by reversing its digits, the sum is 132.

practice.name: Problem 14 (2016 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: How many ways are there to write 2016 as the sum of twos and threes, ignoring order?

practice.name: Problem 19 (2023 AMC 12B)
practice.statement: Each of 2023 balls is randomly placed into one of 3 bins. Which of the following is closest to the probability that each of the bins will contain an odd number of balls?

practice.name: Problem 11 (2018 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: When fair standard 6-sided dice are thrown, the probability that the sum of the numbers on the top faces is 10 can be written as p/6^7, where p is a positive integer. What is p?

practice.name: Problem 9 (2018 AMC 10B)
practice.statement: The faces of each of 7 standard dice are labeled with the integers from 1 to 6. Let P(n) be the probability that when all 7 dice are rolled, the sum of the numbers on the top faces is n. What other sum occurs with the same probability as 10?

practice.name: Problem 17 (2004 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Three friends have a total of $6$ identical pencils, and each one has at least one pencil. In how many ways can this happen?
